evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Apply financial and business context to ensure analytics outputs are accurate, consistent, and meaningful
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals (accuracy, consistency, automated/reusable pipelines) dominate with no velocity/speed language present.
evidence.domain_risk: Familiarity with financial data concepts (e.g., trial balances, P&L, revenue data) and the ability to translate them into analytical solutions
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: The role's core focus is finance-specific data (trial balances, P&L, revenue) for client finance solutions, explicit financial-reporting-adjacent language placing it at the highest risk tier.
evidence.collaboration_width: clients; finance, accounting, or business teams
evidence.data_team_maturity: Work with enterprise data platforms including Microsoft Fabric, Azure, and Snowflake
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Established enterprise data platforms (Fabric, Azure, Snowflake) are named in responsibilities at a mature consulting firm, though the role itself is individual-contributor focused rather than building the foundational data organisation.
evidence.jd_authorship: Use data preparation and automation tools (e.g., Alteryx) to streamline transformations
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific tools (Alteryx, Microsoft Fabric, Azure, Snowflake) and financial concepts (trial balances, P&L) give a concrete picture of the daily work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Familiarity with financial data concepts (e.g., trial balances, P&L, revenue data)
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Finance/accounting data concepts (trial balances, P&L, revenue) and supporting finance/accounting teams dominate the framing, a finance orientation.
evidence.autonomy_level: Collaborate with business and functional stakeholders to understand reporting needs and translate them into data solutions
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role is explicitly framed as a "hands-on contributor" delivering scoped analytics and reporting solutions in response to stakeholder needs, without ownership or direction-setting verbs.
evidence.ai_role: Artificial intelligence (AI) tools are used to support the hiring process in screening, assessing, and/or selecting applicants for this position.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The only AI mention describes the company's use of AI in its own hiring/screening process, not a skill or responsibility expected of the candidate in the role.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: No testing, data quality framework, or data contract language appears anywhere in the extracted JD text.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Apply financial and business context to ensure analytics outputs are accurate, consistent, and meaningful
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Accuracy and consistency of financial analytics outputs are a stated concern, but framed as data quality rather than explicit regulatory or compliance risk.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Design, build, and maintain automated and reusable data pipelines to support analytics and reporting
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role mixes designing new automated pipelines with maintaining and delivering ongoing reporting solutions for client engagements.
evidence.loss_aversion: Apply financial and business context to ensure analytics outputs are accurate, consistent, and meaningful — reflects concern for financial data accuracy rather than explicit regulatory risk.
